network.overall_strategy: Integrate hydrothermal synthesis of key precursors (pyruvate, α-ketoisovalerate) with surface-based Strecker chemistry and catalytic aldol diversification, leveraging mineral catalysts to address the branched aldehyde bottleneck. Combine reductive amination in vents with evaporative concentration and polymerization in surface pools.

reactions.name: CO₂ reduction to acetate
reactions.mechanism: Fischer-Tropsch-type CO₂ hydrogenation on Fe₃S₄ clusters
reactions.reasoning: Huber & Wächtershäuser (1997) demonstrated FeS-mediated CO₂ reduction under hydrothermal conditions

reactions.name: Acetate to pyruvate
reactions.mechanism: Reductive carboxylation via mineral-catalyzed C-C bond formation
reactions.reasoning: Russell et al. (1994) proposed pyruvate synthesis in alkaline vents via Fe₃O₄ catalysis

reactions.name: Aldol diversification to isobutyraldehyde
reactions.mechanism: Acetaldehyde oligomerization on NiS surfaces producing branched aldehydes
reactions.reasoning: Preiner et al. (2023) demonstrated NiS-catalyzed aldol diversification to branched C₄ aldehydes

reactions.name: Strecker synthesis of valine aminonitrile
reactions.mechanism: Imination followed by HCN addition catalyzed by clay surfaces
reactions.reasoning: Keefe et al. (1995) confirmed isobutyraldehyde as Strecker precursor; Miller & Van Trump (1981) mechanism

reactions.name: Aminonitrile hydrolysis to valine
reactions.mechanism: Irreversible nitrile hydrolysis through amide intermediate
reactions.reasoning: Green et al. (2021) characterized aminonitrile stability and hydrolysis kinetics

reactions.name: Pyruvate to α-ketoisovalerate
reactions.mechanism: Mineral-catalyzed condensation analogous to biological acetolactate synthase
reactions.reasoning: Copley et al. (2005) proposed prebiotic α-keto acid chain extension pathways

reactions.name: Reductive amination of α-ketoisovalerate
reactions.mechanism: Fe³⁺/Fe²⁺ redox cycling drives NH₃ incorporation
reactions.reasoning: Barge et al. (2019) demonstrated Fe-oxyhydroxide catalyzed reductive amination; Kaur et al. (2024) Ni-catalyzed version

reactions.name: Photocatalytic HCN formation
reactions.mechanism: UV-driven CH₄/NH₃ photochemistry on semiconductor surfaces
reactions.reasoning: Patel et al. (2015) cyanosulfidic chemistry; Saladino et al. (2006) TiO₂ photocatalysis

pathways.name: Hydrothermal reductive amination
pathways.description: CO₂ → acetate → pyruvate → α-ketoisovalerate → valine via Fe-catalyzed reductive amination

pathways.name: Surface Strecker synthesis
pathways.description: Acetate → isobutyraldehyde → aminonitrile → valine via clay-catalyzed Strecker chemistry

pathways.name: Hybrid hydrothermal-surface
pathways.description: Hydrothermal pyruvate combines with surface-derived isobutyraldehyde for dual synthesis routes

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Hydrothermal reductive amination pathway relies on under-demonstrated α-ketoisovalerate formation (rxn_006). Pyruvate to α-ketoisovalerate lacks explicit prebiotic precedent in literature. Reductive amination (rxn_007) is well-supported by mineral catalysis studies.
suggestions: Specify mineral catalysts for pyruvate carboxylation (rxn_002)
Add experimental evidence for abiotic α-ketoisovalerate synthesis (rxn_006)

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Strecker pathway aligns strongly with literature. Aldol diversification (rxn_003) addresses the critical branched aldehyde bottleneck using surface catalysis as per Preiner 2023. Explicit mineral agents would strengthen environmental realism.
suggestions: Specify NiS catalyst for aldol step (rxn_003)
Add pH constraints for Strecker steps

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Hybrid pathway inherits weaknesses from P1 (rxn_006) but benefits from P2's strong Strecker core. Environmental transitions between hydrothermal and surface phases need explicit concentration mechanisms.
suggestions: Add evaporative concentration step between hydrothermal/surface phases
Clarify α-ketoisovalerate stability during environmental transfer

evaluation.network_feedback: Network effectively addresses the branched aldehyde bottleneck via catalytic aldol diversification (P2) and integrates literature-supported mechanisms. Weakness in α-ketoisovalerate synthesis (P1/P3) and under-specified mineral catalysis lowers scoring. Approved due to P2's viability.
